objective: Decide whether H1's repaired connector graph is public-safe and cueable enough to clear the access/cue blocker, without promoting route cards.

public_source_references.role: Official Avimor trail names, seasonal-closure framing, trail rules, and public-facing map link.
Public ArcGIS Hike/Bike layer used for named-trail open/closed and hike-bike eligibility checks.

Public map/source corroboration that Twisted Spring connects the trailhead area to Spring Valley Creek.

leg_by_leg_cueability.title: Access to Twisted Spring
leg_by_leg_cueability.field_cue_name: Avimor Spring Valley Creek parking -> McLeod Way Greenbelt/Twisted Spring Trail #8
public_legal_evidence: Opaque OSM connector is within 0.0001 mi of Avimor ArcGIS open Hike/Bike trail McLeod Way Greenbelt (A34).
OSM connector tags are highway=footway surface=concrete access=None foot=None.
No access=no, access=private, foot=no, or foot=private tag was present in the local connector source.
Opaque OSM connector is within 0.002 mi of Avimor ArcGIS open Hike/Bike trail McLeod Way Greenbelt (A34).
OSM connector tags are highway=footway surface=concrete access=None foot=None.

OSM connector tags are highway=footway surface=None access=None foot=None.
leg_by_leg_cueability.expected_field_landmark_or_junction: Leave the lot on the paved greenbelt/park path, then pick up the signed Twisted Spring Trail #8 / Spring Valley Creek trailhead area.
leg_by_leg_cueability.fallback_if_missed: If the Twisted Spring trailhead/bridge is not obvious from the lot, do not improvise through homes; return to the car and use H2 or the current microcards.
leg_by_leg_cueability.status_reason: All non-official connector pieces can be cued as named trail, public road, or nearby open Hike/Bike trail without relying on opaque OSM IDs.

promotion_readiness.not_promotion_note: This clears or classifies the access/cue review only. It does not remove the existing Harlow/Avimor route cards from the active field packet.

frame_shift.artifact_contract: Public-safe access/cue review for the candidate route, not a route promotion.
frame_shift.downstream_question: Can a runner leave the Avimor parking lot, follow named/signed public routes without opaque OSM IDs, and return to the car without hidden private/no-foot geometry?
frame_shift.negative_space: This does not prove day-of trail conditions, live signage, or full field-packet readiness after route-card replacement.
frame_shift.decision: keep-gated until route-card promotion and recertification even when the access/cue gate passes.
